title: Embeddings, Vector Spaces, Similarity, and Model Contracts · Behavior Case: Rag Similarity Metric
summary: A team mixes query vectors from one model with document vectors from another, changes cosine to dot product, truncates dimensions, and interprets closeness as factual support. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the similarity metric increment in embedding-space and similarity audit. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.
Enforce model, revision, dimension, dtype, prompt, and preprocessing identity for every query and document vector.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example RAG engineering team handles case 6004 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to trace embedding space, dimensions, similarity, normalization, and model-version boundaries; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract. A defensible solution applies Rag Similarity Metric, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to embedding-space and similarity audit.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-embeddings-vector-semantics-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-embeddings-vector-semantics-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_embeddings_vector_semantics_5(query: tuple[float, ...] = (1.0, 0.0, 1.0), document: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 0.0, 0.5), dimensions: int = 3, same_model: bool = True, normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if dimensions <= 0 or len(query) != dimensions or len(document) != dimensions:
        return False, 0.0, "dimension-contract"
    if not same_model:
        return False, 0.0, "embedding-space"
    dot = sum(left * right for left, right in zip(query, document, strict=True))
    query_norm = sum(value * value for value in query) ** 0.5
    document_norm = sum(value * value for value in document) ** 0.5
    if query_norm == 0.0 or document_norm == 0.0:
        return False, 0.0, "zero-vector"
    cosine = dot / (query_norm * document_norm)
    if normalized and abs(query_norm - 1.0) > 1e-6:
        return False, cosine, "normalization-claim"
    assert -1.0 <= cosine <= 1.0
    return True, round(cosine, 4), "cosine-evidence"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python RAG decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected query or corpus case, then state which native model, vector-store, provider, network, load, privacy, security, recovery, or deployment claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Similarity Metric: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 5290 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to trace embedding space, dimensions, similarity, normalization, and model-version boundaries; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Similarity Metric model · Theory Rag Similarity Metric.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Similarity Metric model · Theory Rag Similarity Metric, keep Rag Similarity Metric connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Similarity Metric model · Theory Rag Similarity Metric, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Similarity Metric precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Similarity Metric model · Theory Rag Similarity Metric, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 5290 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to trace embedding space, dimensions, similarity, normalization, and model-version boundaries; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Similarity Metric model · Theory Rag Similarity Metric, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Cosine similarity, dot product, and Euclidean distance always produce identical rankings.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: rag-similarity-metric-l5a5y7(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_rag_similarity_metric_l5a5y7\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?len\(query\) != dimensions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not same_model)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?zip\(query, document, strict=True\))(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?query_norm == 0.0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
